item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This just barely doesn't work for me. The reaction of the Assistant Researcher to knowing her faces matches to a 150 year missing victim is baffling, and disconnected enough to just leave me confused. It's disconnected from the rest of the SCP at a pivotal moment of the climax, so I finished the SCP scratching my head.

The effect of the line is to make you think that John has a personnel connection to this corpse, and that it shouldn't be here. But then the end comes, and both of those are proven wrong, seemingly. Why is he surprised to see an old missing corpse? How does he recognize it right awya?

Which is really, really, quite the shame, because I did love this otherwise. It's atmospheric, creepy and everything else about it works excellently. It just whiffs on the ending too hard.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think they didn't -put- her back down. I think the weather won, given the effort the area was doing to keep her hidden.

They lost, she was underground again.

Keeping her out, anyway, could've led to permanently-altered weather in the desert area… quite hard to explain!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read the addendum before I finished reading the description. “Oh, she hasn’t aged,” I thought. And then I scrolled up and…

Investigation into which biological functions are hindered is currently underway.

Shit.

Apart from the fact that I think there needs to be a longer interview log explaining why they put her back IN the ground, and what happened immediately after they dug her out, I think this is underrated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The idea and description are interesting, however some things in the skip don't make sense (Why did they bury her again? Why didn't they try unearthing the things again, with more preparation? Why was this guy so shook by the person being unearthed looking like someone he hasn't seen in his whole life and didn't even know existed until that point?! [For example: And then I opened the car and… dramatic 10 second pause the guy inside was SOME RANDOM GUY I HAD NEVER SEEN BEFORE! dramatic music].

Much of this doesn't make any sense and squanders the beautiful description you had going. Downvoted

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Read this because it was linked in chat.

It's pretty much okay. I agree with A Blessed Feline's comment above — that, and the artificial dramatic pause to repeat an obvious line smacks of contrivance. That, and the fact that given the anomaly you've set up, I don't know why the Foundation wouldn't investigate all this further, using robots and such. After all, a trap like this could easily catch Foundation personnel too. (They can fail to find anything useful to help them without sending more people in — dangerous — but that would probably be noted in the article. If they succeed, but there's some other unexpected and maybe horrifying effect if you want to keep the horror theme — that could also be interesting.)

As it stands, this is too standard for me. It's decently written though, so no vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's definitely the latent claustrophobia talking, but this one chilled me. Reminds me a bit of The Devil's Tramping Grounds from my home state. Supposedly it's a haunted campsite where nothing grows, the scent of sulfur lingners on the air, and campers who stay the night find their sleeping bags mysteriously moved outside the clearing in the morning…

In reality it's a place for teenagers to get drunk, but I always enjoyed the legend.

Anyway, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: While the actual anomaly it kind of interesting, the addendum seemed to be just a clumsy way to introduce SCP-2392-A, which I can't see why there wouldn't be anything about in the Description. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Down vote for now because it's so simple but the idea and part of the execution is really really solid. You could easally add in two or three more logs or add conflicting accounts or hell, even create a PoI Anonomous Serial killer and turn that into the SCP.

I love this idea and I do believe it deserves to be on the main list, but not as is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the "stock creepy place" aspect of it, maybe I'm weird. I didn't see the original version of this article, but this version has earned my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Much better, although I'd move the abduction forward from 1840 to perhaps 1870. More historically probable that photos of such a woman would have existed for the Foundation to find.

edit: also, I think it's important that the Foundation manage to have taken a photo of this woman's face before she was swallowed up again. Otherwise it's just a description.

item.title: Re: Update post
item.content:encoded:
Fine. I suppose you have updated it enough and polished the rough stone, so to speak. It's gonna keep the baggage of its initial form, so it'll take some time for it to go to the positives (provided that more people like it than dislike it, of course).

You may want to announce it on the Updates thread in the forums.

item.title: Update post
item.content:encoded: This post designates the uploading of Version 2 of SCP-2392.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I actually find this idea really good, but it could have a little bit more of the "whoa, freaky anomalous stuff" feeling. Upvoted to stave off deletion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Echoing what others have said: workable, but needs a push to really work.

To elaborate: Right now, it's a description of a stock scary place. The article is working against you here because the combination of describing the scary place and having the place not be too unique to begin with causes the viewer to not really feel what's actually so scary about it. Rather than feeling the atmosphere you want them to feel, they just feel "yeah, this is a stock scary place, I'm familiar with this trope", exacerbated by the pragmatic tone.

You clearly know the pragmatic tone is inimical to typical scares, because you include the D-Class testimony specifically to try to counteract that. But, by the time the reader gets to that point, the damage is already done. To fix this, you need something that isn't just unnatural when you're in it but is unnatural to describe, even in a clinical tone. Just saying it gives you a feeling of dread won't make the reader feel dread. There needs to be a more unique brand of horror for it to work in a clinical context, here.

POST-UPDATE EDIT: This works a lot better. There is still some baggage moving me to a novote, but it's an improvement overall. One thing, though:

[Assistant Researcher John pauses for thirty seconds.]

This is an absurdly long time to pause. As I once learned in some high-school drama class, a one second pause is a short breath, two seconds is dramatic, three seconds or more is just awkward. And you have ten times that. I think ten seconds would be a more reasonable time for him to be silent before the other guy decides to move forwards.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Exactly.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neutral-voting, because this has a lot of potential, and almost works for me. Definitely ditch addendum 1; it's at best a distraction, and at worst a tension-killer. It doesn't tell us anything that we can't infer from the rest of the traits (ie: the place is dang freaky), and it steals a lot of impact from that nice little understated gut punch in addendum 2.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's a good start, but it doesn't go anywhere or do anything.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one who feels that it would work as a part of the Log Of Anomalous Locations? It just seems like something that belongs there and not as a full blown SCP. It's just got that vibe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's the story with the missing woman? You don't have to spell it out, but if it's going to be presented as the reason for the haunting, we should know something about it.

I don't think we need the first addendum. The sense of dread is established. We'd need to see something new there.

You could introduce the crying earlier, perhaps? Reveal something known about the location (like the missing person), and finish with revealing the face, who says something that twists the situation further. Something like that.

Ramp up the tension with the events, and end it with us positive that we've only scratched the surface.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now I ask myself: What was the point of the first addendum again? It feels like it's trying to assert its scariness when the article pretty much tore apart the scariness through the power of scientific methodology.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This feels more like an anomalous location than an SCP, since it doesn't actually do anything except for spooking people and killing plants. Addendum 2 hints at something bigger, but I wish it was explored more, maybe the Foundation actually discovering/triggering something while poking around in the center.

One other question on my mind is, if it kills plants in the area, does long-term exposure do anything to humans or animals, aside from stress and whatnot?

Edit: And it seems you expanded on Addendum 2 while I was writing this. I feel it could still use more details to it, but now I'm getting a vague "punishment for SCP-2392-A" vibe, which I like.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nothing to say really although it's kinda short, I would give you a positive vote if you put an image of that location. Meh neutral vote for now. I'll think about this later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is literally just a stock description of a haunted place. Not to say it isn't creepy, but it feels like it needs a push more from "mildly unsettling" to "oh fuck what's wrong with this". Neutral vote, but looks pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I accept most of your description, I find the test subject's statement to be overly convenient. You can do this in a few ways:

  • Actually reveal a dead body hidden in that area. It would be better if you can play around with the typical "dead body found by supernatural forces" concept.
  • Have different people give differing and contradicting accounts of what they think happen to the area

Beyond that, neutral vote for now.

This is my attempt at short horror.

Inspiration behind SCP-2392
2392, Soudan and Ike

Got inspiration for this during my camping trip - getting late at night and we were looking for a place to camp, so we pulled over at a spot called Soudan Station.
Creepiest place I've ever been to - right next to the highway but hidden by really dense shrubs, only lights within a few twenty kilometers was our own headlights, and even those seemed like they couldn't penetrate the darkness. It was dead silent too, but in a really unsettling way. We let off some fireworks and shoved off - as we left, a nearby sign was illuminated by our headlights, the sign of some arse writing on it with blood red paint becoming evident.

So I wrote this as we went to the next place.

Check out this awesome reading by ReadOut!
AND this translation on the Japanese wiki!
